rows.notes: Top-line title. Handwriting is clear and legible; no amounts on this line. Vertical ruling lines are not used for headers, and the words read unambiguously as 'Receipts by Biddulph Cocks'.

Second header line appears to read '& Co. Bankers in London 1796'. Date '1796' is clear; ampersand and 'Co. Bankers in London' are legible though slightly abbreviated. No monetary values on this line.

rows.description: 1/2 years Divd on 4108.17.6 cons 3 per Cent
rows.notes: Description reads as a half-year dividend on '4108.17.6' cons (3% indicated). Amount aligned to the rightmost £/s/d columns using the clear total line as an alignment guide. Pounds (61), shillings (12) and pence (7) digits are distinct; slight uncertainty on some numerals in the principal figure '4108.17.6' but the monetary columns are well aligned and the pence digit is clearly a '7'.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year dividend on 3% Consols stock, amounting to £61 12s 7d.

rows.notes: Reads as half-year interest on '546.0.9' at 4%. Amount columns located by vertical ruled lines; pounds '10' and shillings '18' are clear, pence looks like '4'. No fraction symbol after pence. Minor ambiguity in the manuscript '546.0.9' (dots/commas) but the monetary figures are unambiguous.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year interest on 4% stock, amounting to £10 18s 4d.

rows.notes: Second similar half-year dividend on the 4108.17.6 cons (3%). Amount repeats the earlier 61-12-7. Alignment and digits match the earlier dividend row; small uncertainty only because the line is slightly lighter but numerals match.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year dividend on 3% Consols stock, amounting to £61 12s 7d.

rows.notes: Repeat of the half-year 4% interest on 546.0.9. Amount 10-18-4 aligns with the previous 4% entry above; digits are clear though the ink is slightly lighter here, hence moderate confidence.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year interest on 4% stock, amounting to £10 18s 4d.

rows.description: Excheq'r Annuity for a year due July 5-96
rows.notes: Reads 'Excheq'r Annuity for a year due July 5-96' (abbreviation for Exchequer). Amount 50-0-0 is clearly written and aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. The date 'July 5-96' is legible and corroborates the header year.
rows.english_description: Exchequer annuity for the year, due 5 July 1796.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Exchequer annuity for the year due 5 July 1796, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Final boxed total at right reads '195. 1. 10' (195 pounds, 1 shilling, 10 pence). Used the ruled vertical lines to determine pound/shilling/pence boundaries; all three digits are clearly formed and validate (shillings <20, pence <12). No fraction symbol present.
